summary: n8n-nodes-net-utils exfiltrates host recon and opens reverse shell to 103.27.109.184:8893 on require
details: The package presents itself as an n8n community node (NetUtilsNode.js is an empty stub with no functionality), but index.js runs a top-level IIFE that executes on require. The IIFE runs `hostname`, `hostname -I`, routing tables, and `env`, scans an internal Docker subnet, and streams the collected host identifiers, full environment variables, and network reconnaissance data over a raw TCP socket to the hardcoded remote host 103.27.109.184:8893. The same file spawns `/bin/bash -i` and pipes its stdin/stdout/stderr through a second TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8893, giving the remote party an interactive shell on the installer's host every time the module is loaded. The n8n node facade is a cover story; the module's actual purpose is host compromise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
